local.change_request: Improve quality across the entire repo (src/, tests/, docs/, scripts/, .abtree/) by raising DRY, SRP, coupling, and cohesion scores against their default 0.7 thresholds. Out-of-scope: docs/.vitepress/* (already cleaned), node_modules, plans/, bugs/.

observations.issue: ExecutionStore.getLocal/setLocal/replaceLocal/deleteLocal are mirrored almost exactly by getGlobal/setGlobal/replaceGlobal/deleteGlobal — only the scope key differs

getRuntimeStatus/setRuntimeStatus, getRuntimeStep/setRuntimeStep, getRuntimeRetryCount/incrementRuntimeRetryCount all follow readDoc -> mutate doc.runtime[map][key] -> writeDoc; the readDoc+writeDoc bookend is duplicated 6 times
cmdNext and cmdSubmit have repeated ExecutionStore.update({phase:idle, cursor:null}); rebuildMermaid(id); out({...}); return; blocks at every terminal-ish path
cmdLocalRead and cmdGlobalRead have identical shape (path? out({path,value}) : out(scope)) — could share a helper
Each test that drives an execution repeats the create -> next (gate) -> submit success boilerplate

cmdNext now handles four distinct phases: terminal status check (added in this session), protocol gate, evaluating-phase replay, performing-phase replay, plus fresh tickRoot. Each has its own reasons to change (gate semantics, cursor encoding, runtime tick contract).
cmdSubmit handles the protocol gate flow AND the regular performing-phase flow in one function with a sentinel branch. The two paths share no state.
Mixes loading + dereferencing ($ref), normalisation, the runtime tick loop, and getters/setters into one module. Tree loading rarely changes for the same reason as the tick algorithm.
ExecutionStore object exposes scope helpers (local/global), runtime helpers (status/step/retry), and crud (find/list/create/update/delete) all together. Three change axes, one struct.

Cursor encoding (JSON-stringified {path, step} objects) leaks: cmdNext does JSON.parse(doc.cursor) then accesses cursor.path/cursor.step directly. The producer (tickRoot writing JSON.stringify({path, step})) and consumer (cmdNext) are coupled by a string format with no schema.
rebuildMermaid(executionId) is called by hand after almost every state-mutation site. Forgetting one is silently wrong; this is an invariant the store could enforce instead of the caller.
ExecutionStore.setRuntimeStatus / setRuntimeStep are designed for the tick engine but are public on the same object as scope helpers. Callers can reach into runtime state from anywhere.
Combines tree YAML loading + $ref dereferencing, runtime tick algorithm, and per-node helpers (getNodeAtPath, setNodeResult, setStepIndex). Two genuinely separable concerns under one module heading.

ExecutionStore lumps scope helpers (local/global) with runtime helpers (status/step/retry) and the crud surface (find/list/create/update/delete) into one object. The runtime helpers are tick-engine internals that callers in commands.ts can also reach.
Filesystem path constants + ensureDir + SKILL_TARGETS / SkillVariant / SkillScope live together. The skill installer target table belongs with the install command, not with directory paths.

local.report: Baseline: dry=0.70 srp=0.65 coupling=0.70 cohesion=0.70 (all thresholds 0.70). 13 candidate findings deduplicated to 11 — see refactor_queue. SRP is the only metric below threshold pre-refactor; DRY/coupling/cohesion sit at 0.70 with headroom.

dry: Fowler refactoring catalogue: Extract Function, Extract Variable, Parameterize Function. Key heuristic: the duplicated code must be doing the *same* thing for the *same* reason — incidental similarity is not duplication.
TypeScript-specific: prefer a single function with a discriminating parameter over generic type-parameter gymnastics; readability over cleverness.
srp: Robert Martin: a class/function should have only one reason to change, where reason = stakeholder-facing concern. Don’t split aggressively just to satisfy a metric — splits that fragment a coherent flow can hurt cohesion.
TypeScript pattern: extract handler functions for distinct phases of a long switch/conditional. Keep the dispatch site small and single-purpose.
coupling: Hide the encoding behind a typed boundary: replace ad-hoc JSON.stringify/parse pairs with a small class or pair of pure encode/decode functions. Eliminates the implicit contract.
Move cross-cutting invariants (logging, cache rebuild, audit) into the layer that owns the data, not every caller. Decorator pattern or wrapper functions inside the store.
cohesion: Cohesion improves when modules contain functions that call each other and share private state. A module whose exports do not interact is a sign that one module should be two.
Co-locate types with the code that uses them. Generic util modules (paths.ts holding skill targets) tend to attract miscellany.

done_log.summary: Collapse ExecutionStore.{getLocal,setLocal,replaceLocal,deleteLocal} and the global-scope mirrors into a single scope-parameterised pair. Trivial mechanical change.
Move rebuildMermaid invocation into ExecutionStore.update / setLocal etc so callers cannot forget. Eliminates the cross-cutting invariant scattered through commands.ts.
Split cmdNext into focused helpers: terminal-status check, protocol-gate emission, evaluating-phase replay, performing-phase replay, fresh tickRoot. Each helper has one reason to change.
Extract handleProtocolSubmit from cmdSubmit so the gate flow and the regular performing flow are separate functions sharing only the not-found guard.

Move runtime helpers (getRuntimeStatus, setRuntimeStatus, step, retry, resetRuntimeSubtree) out of ExecutionStore into a separate RuntimeStore in src/runtime-store.ts, used only by tree.ts. Tightens access; subsumes I2 and I12.
Replace JSON.stringify/parse cursor convention with a typed Cursor encode()/decode() in one place.
Move SKILL_TARGETS / SkillVariant / SkillScope from src/paths.ts into src/skills.ts.

global.test_command: the command that runs the project's full regression test suite (e.g. 'bun test', 'pnpm test')
